reviews.author: Rhys Scarrott
reviews.text: 4th Largest transnational border waterfall in the world. Review is for Vietnam side. For 40k it would have to be one of the best and cheapest entry’s anywhere in the WORLD 🌍. It’s such an amazing site to see. There’s so many waterfalls to. Capture its incredible. Small boat rides up to the falls can be taken for another small fee of 50k. I took the boat all by myself and paid only 100k and went for 15-20mins. Well worth it. Obviously the otherside being China is quite busy though the Vietnamese side was very quiet before lunchtime hours.

reviews.author: Theo Dangla
reviews.text: Absolutely stunning natural wonder at the Vietnam–China border. Ban Gioc Waterfall is one of the most beautiful and dramatic waterfalls I’ve seen — wide, multi-tiered cascades surrounded by lush karst scenery that feel almost otherworldly. The boat ride near the base brings you right into the mist, and the peaceful, uncrowded setting made it a highlight of my trip. A must-visit if you love nature and photography.

reviews.author: Kevin G
reviews.text: Ban Gioc Waterfall (called Detian Waterfall on the Chinese side) is one of the most spectacular natural sights in Vietnam — and in all of Southeast Asia — for several reasons: 🌟 What makes Ban Gioc Waterfall special 1. It’s one of the largest waterfalls in Asia Ban Gioc is often listed among the top 4 largest transnational waterfalls in the world (alongside Niagara, Iguazu, and Victoria), based on width. • The falls extend about 300 meters across, creating a dramatic multi-tiered cascade. 2. It straddles an international border The waterfall sits between Vietnam and China, with the Quây Sơn River forming the border. • The left side is Vietnam, • The right side is China. This unique location gives the place a fascinating mix of cultures, landscapes, and history. 3. Multi-tiered, terraced design Unlike single-drop waterfalls, Ban Gioc has three major tiers, creating a layered, photogenic effect. In rainy season, the falls are thunderous and wide; in dry season, they break into dozens of silky streams. 4. Dramatic karst mountain setting It sits in a valley surrounded by limestone karsts, rice fields, and small ethnic villages. The scenery is often described as “like stepping into a painting.” 5. Peaceful, uncrowded atmosphere (compared to major global waterfalls)

reviews.author: Giang Huynh
reviews.text: I love my country where the nature is so beautiful. The falls are like the silk bridal veil. Just sitting and listening to the sound of the nature, I felt relaxed and revived. One star was off beacause the local people selling goodies tried to rip off tourists. I wanted to support them but too overpricing set me off.

reviews.author: Ksenia Kaganer
reviews.text: Beautiful Waterfall, nice to walk around and explore also the smaller waterfalls. We visited early February. Ticket price 40k for adult, option to take a bout closer to the fall for 50k. Arrive at least 16:30, 17:00 they already started to close. It was nice to see also things lighting up when it’s get darker.

reviews.author: yanerps
reviews.text: This has gotta be my favourite attraction in Northern Vietnam 🇻🇳 It's really cool to experience being near the waterfall by taking the raft. You can see the China border and the tourists right across the river doing the same activity. FEES 💸 Motorcycle parking: 10,000 VND - the market vendors will approach you when they see you parked along the waterfall entrace. It's annoyingly intrusive but that's just the way things operate I guess 🤷🏻‍♀️ Entrance fee: 40,000 VND / pax Waterfall Raft: 50,000 VND / pax Went there on a weekday so there weren't many people. It's very peaceful 🙂‍↕️ even if it's just the 2 of us on the raft, they move right away without waiting the seats to be filled up.

reviews.author: Geraldine Gut
reviews.text: Super nice been there during Tet Holiday not many tourists. We didn't went on the Boat. Im my opinion its not necessary. You can walk pretty close to the waterfall. It's super mystical. 🧚🏼‍♀️🦋✨️ Side note Bathing is forbidden.!😉

reviews.author: Meital Zion
reviews.text: Ban Gioc Waterfalls are one of the most impressive natural sites in Vietnam. The waterfalls are wide and powerful, with open and beautiful scenery, and the surrounding area is well maintained and pleasant to visit. Access to the site is relatively easy, and the visit offers a calm and immersive nature experience. A recommended destination for anyone seeking striking landscapes and a special stop in northern Vietnam.

reviews.author: Sim Sim
reviews.text: Should you come here or not? If you expect Niagrafalls..don’t come here, but if you are in this region, just take an hourt to visit this place. Real nice waterfalls. Come here early in the day before the waterfall get in the shadow (i think betweem 9.00 and 14.00-ish) Costs: 40k p.p. Entrance, 50k p.p. Boat, 10k parking for scooter: if you come with 2 persons: 190k = €6,23. just pay and enjoy, they realy try to make it a good Experience. There are a lot of locals selling all kinds of things to tourists, you can even get dressed up for a fotoshoot in Vietnamees outfit. Just support.

reviews.author: Oscar Loison
reviews.text: Incredible waterfalls, even in dry season there is a lot of water It’s juste sad that ten boat are ruining a little bit the view (50k to go on them) And you can clearly see the difference between the Vietnamese side and the Chinese side, the infrastructure are much better on the Chinese side…

reviews.author: Bellie B
reviews.text: Horrifically polluted waterfalls and viewpoints closed off. There was a marathon the day before but that is no excuse, all events should have waste management plans. Furthermore much of the garbage is old and in the water (cannot blame the event for that). With thousands of visitors each week the tourism department is making a lot of money from this. I think they should actually use that money to clean this place and make it nice to visit or else why are you charging entry at all? Of course the waterfalls are spectacular but I'm giving it 1 star for management. Absolutely disgusting.

reviews.author: Aleksandar Mandzhukov
reviews.text: It was majestic. It worth the 8 hours drive. I love it. One of the greatest waterfall in the world. Just inform in-front when there will be water. Because on the other side they stoped it.

reviews.author: Mikey P
reviews.text: Amazing views, cheap ticket for entry can buy inside. Don't feel like you need to get one of those boats out to the waterfalls since you can walk along the river bank quite close to soak it in. Also cool to be able to see china right across the other side. After checking the waterfalls out, make sure to go up to the Buddhist pagoda on the hill nearby for an amazing above view of the falls and the entire valley you worn regret it - some ladies at the road might try and tell you you can't drive up the road to the temple in the hope you park in front of their stalls but you can no problem to save yourself a big steep walk - just be careful and go slow on the ride up & down.

reviews.author: Hien Nguyen
reviews.text: Cao Bang left me genuinely amazed from the moment I arrived. Tucked away in Vietnam’s far north, this land feels untouched, pure, and breathtakingly beautiful. The journey itself was already a delight – winding mountain roads, fresh cool air, and endless views of rolling hills fading into the horizon. But nothing prepared me for the sight of Ban Gioc Waterfall. Standing before it, I was overwhelmed by its sheer size and power. The water cascaded down in layers, white and foamy, into a jade-green pool, surrounded by lush greenery. It was like stepping into a scene from a dream. Everywhere in Cao Bang seemed to hold its own magic. The Quay Son River flowed calmly through golden rice fields, reflecting the towering limestone mountains like a giant natural mirror. Villages dotted along the way felt peaceful and welcoming, with traditional stilt houses and warm smiles from the locals. The air was crisp and clean, carrying the scent of fresh earth and blooming flowers. What surprised me most was how raw and untouched everything felt. There were no crowds, no rush – just nature in its purest form. I spent hours simply sitting by the river, watching the sunlight dance on the water, feeling a deep sense of peace I rarely find elsewhere. Leaving Cao Bang, I couldn’t help but feel grateful to have witnessed such beauty. It’s the kind of place that stays with you, not just in photographs, but in your heart – a reminder that some of the world’s most stunning treasures are found where few have yet to wander.

reviews.author: Leon Wüst
reviews.text: It is cheap (40k) and kind of nice, but if you have seen a few waterfalls in your life you won't be astonished. They sell lots of stuff in front and you will be forced to walk through all the shops which sell exactly same stuff (chinese cigarettes, alcohol, and souvenirs and also vietnamese souvenirs and clothing). Prices at the shops are reasonable

reviews.author: K J
reviews.text: I visited here with a guide I found on get your guide, trip advisor also a good app to compare day tours and multi-day tours with. Best place I've visited all trip. Tour guide fantastic and driver very safe.

reviews.author: TravelbyTin
reviews.text: The biggest waterfall in Việt Nam. Great view and nice place, high recommended.

reviews.author: Teck Kim Chia
reviews.text: The view of Bản Giốc Waterfall, nestled along the border between Vietnam and China is simply breathtaking. This natural wonder is one of the most majestic and scenic spots in Northern Vietnam. The cascading waters, surrounded by lush emerald green rice fields, create a serene and charming atmosphere. The waterfall itself is massive and powerful, yet incredibly peaceful to watch. The mist rising from the falls adds a magical feel. Whether viewed from the river below on a bamboo raft or from one of the nearby viewpoints, the scenery is nothing short of spectacular. However, the park is relatively under developed compared to other tourist destinations, which actually adds to its charm. It feels authentic, quiet, and not overly commercialized. The locals are warm and welcoming, and there's a sense of calm that stays with you long after you've left. Getting there however is a bit challenging, the road journey from Hanoi Airport to the waterfall is around 11 hours if you add in stopovers for meals and toilet breaks, but the stunning landscapes along the way make it worthwhile. It will be a good idea to find a nearby accommodation to rest for the night to rest and head for the waterfall the next day. Highly recommended for nature lovers, photographers, or anyone looking to experience the raw beauty of Vietnam. I can assure you it is all worth the effort

reviews.author: Mariana
reviews.text: Absolutely beautiful! Come before 10 am to avoid crowds that arrive by buses. And it was great to see China so close :)

reviews.author: Rosie Cole
reviews.text: We loved these waterfalls. It wasn't busy when we visited, it definitely felt like they were in the process of building some more paths, some are half built but not a problem. A bargain for only 40k each. Plus 10k to park our scooter outside.

reviews.author: Josh Lawrence
reviews.text: Beautiful! Recommend to visit early in the morning, 8-10am to avoid heavy crowds. Very cool to look across the river and see China!

reviews.author: Văn Thịnh Nguyễn
reviews.text: 🌟 Rating: 5/5 📍 Ban Gioc Waterfall – A Breathtaking Masterpiece of Nature Ban Gioc is one of the most stunning waterfalls in Southeast Asia, and witnessing it in person feels like stepping into a dream. The cascading water stretches wide across multiple levels, tumbling down from the cliffs into the emerald river below, all framed by majestic limestone mountains and lush green fields. ✨ Highlights: Magnificent natural scenery that looks like a real-life painting. Fresh, cool air – a peaceful escape from the city. Countless photo spots, especially beautiful during sunrise or sunset. You can take a bamboo raft ride close to the base of the falls – an unforgettable experience! If you're exploring Northern Vietnam, Ban Gioc Waterfall is a must-see for nature lovers and photography enthusiasts alike.

reviews.author: Philip Blair
reviews.text: Impressive Waterfall with the added frisson of putting you literally within touching distance of China. Your ticket enables you to be a passenger on one of those raft-boats that will take you on a gentle circuit from the Vietnamese side, up close to the falls and then within 3 metres of the Chinese side of the river before returning. An interesting and enjoyable experience. Mildly annoying gauntlet of traders in and out but overall worth the time.

reviews.author: Ruth W
reviews.text: amazing scenery, amazing nature. the river separates the vietnam and china borders. it was a little slippery and muddy because of the rainy (and typhoon) season in vietnam so be careful when walking. it wasn't overly touristy when i visited the waterfalls. the entrance fee cost 40k VND and an additional 50k VND would get you a raft ride towards the waterfalls and back. just FYI: our boat didn't drop us off at the chinese side and im not sure whether that'd have required a different ticket.

reviews.author: Andrea Teh
reviews.text: Visited in early Nov when weather is alright, but do expect some rainy days. Experience was just ok. Reached in the morning at 10am. Water flow was strong as it rained the day before we came. The road condition from Meo Vac to Cao Bang was quite bad, landslides at quite a lot of places. We were stuck for 2.5hrs due to it. When it's not raining, road is dusty. You'll need an experience local driver to travel around North Vietnam, best by car.

reviews.author: John Winter
reviews.text: This place is absolutely stunning—the most beautiful waterfalls I’ve seen in Vietnam. The sheer amount of water flowing over the multi-tiered falls is incredible, and the setting feels like something out of a postcard. I took a bamboo raft ride right up close to the base, which gave a whole new perspective of its size and power. What really amazed me was how many separate falls are in one spot—dozens of them spilling down through lush greenery. It’s surreal seeing the Vietnam–China border marked by such a natural wonder. From where I stood and snapped my photos, the views were unforgettable. Water was cascading over moss-covered rocks, mist rising up into the air, and boats drifting towards the falls. The water below was a clean greenish-blue, and the surrounding limestone peaks just added to the drama. There were spots where the water pooled perfectly still before continuing downstream. The walkways and bridges gave access to all the best viewpoints without spoiling the natural vibe. If you’re anywhere in northern Vietnam—especially around Cao Bằng—this place should be at the top of your list. Note you cannot use drones here due to proximity to the Chinese border.

reviews.author: M D
reviews.text: Best waterfall ever been to!! Wow! beautiful ride there and back also. Go early morning to avoid the masses

reviews.author: Ahmed Shan
reviews.text: Location is a bit remote cause it's on the border but don't let that deter you from coming here. Absolutely stunning views if you like nature. And if you do come here take the river cruise.

reviews.author: Voyager
reviews.text: One of the most breathtaking places I’ve ever visited!” Ban Gioc Waterfall is an absolute gem and a must-visit if you’re in northern Vietnam. The power and beauty of the cascading water surrounded by lush green mountains is something you have to see to believe. I took a bamboo raft ride close to the waterfall — the view was spectacular, and the mist on my face made the experience even more magical. Despite being a bit far from Hanoi, the peaceful scenery and local charm make it totally worth the journey. A perfect spot for photography, nature lovers, or anyone looking to experience Vietnam’s raw natural beauty. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Tomas Ho
reviews.text: Fabulous! Better than expected. Unfortunately the viewing was confined to the Vietnamese side which is a wide expanse of land at the foot of the fall while the high vantage point from the skywalk is over at the Chinese side across the border.
